title: System for diagnosing and treating microvascular obstructions
abstract: Devices for the diagnosis and treatment of microvascular obstruction (MVO) and other dysfunctional diseases of the microvasculature of many organs, including the heart. The present subject matter provides novel devices and methods to detect and measure or treat MVO in real time during scenarios such as invasive angiographic/therapeutic procedures. Such procedures include therapy for organ systems including the heart (acute myocardial infarction - primary percutaneous coronary intervention (PPCI)), brain stroke (CVA), bowel ischemia/infarction, pulmonary emboli/infarction, critical limb ischemia/infarction, renal ischemia/infarction, and others. Using methods of the invention, a system comprising specialized infusion and sensing catheter, diagnostic agents, therapeutic agents, and control console with specialized algorithms can both diagnose and treat MVO by eliminating the microvascular clot and debris causing the obstruction. The techniques involve a combination of novel devices, methods, and software to simultaneously diagnose and treat MVO.
claims: Claims Claims: 1. A system for delivering a controlled flow infusion comprising: a guidewire; a percutaneous transvascular catheter including an occlusion balloon near a distal end of the catheter, the catheter including a lumen configured for infusing an infusate to a location at or near the distal end, the catheter including a guidewire lumen adapted to receive the guidewire; a pump in communication with the catheter; a plurality of infusates in communication with the pump; and a computerized controller configured to be communicate with the pump and configured to be coupled to the catheter, the controller adapted to execute instructions that allow for programmable delivery of pulses of one or more of the plurality of infusates to the catheter. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the guidewire includes a pressure sensor, and the pressure sensor is configured to measure pressure. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the catheter includes a catheter pressure sensor, and the catheter pressure sensor is configured to measure pressure. 4. The system of claim 2, wherein the controller adapted to receive pressure measurements from the pressure sensor. 5. The system of claim 3, wherein the controller adapted to receive pressure measurements from the catheter pressure sensor. 6. The system of any of the foregoing claims, wherein the controller is configured to adjust delivery of one or more of the plurality of infusates based on one or more of the pressure measurements. 7. The system of any of the foregoing claims wherein the catheter includes a pores in an outer wall of the catheter near the distal end of the catheter. 8. The system of claim 7, wherein the catheter includes a cap. 9. The system of claim 8 wherein the catheter includes a pressure sensing chamber disposed near the distal end of the catheter. 10. The system of any of the preceding claims where the catheter includes jets. 11. The system of any of the preceding claims where the catheter includes antegrade jets, retrograde jets, or both antegrade and retrograde jets. 12. The system of any of the preceding claims wherein the catheter has a tapered distal end. 13. The system of any of the preceding claims wherein the pump receives at least one signal from one or more sensors disposed near the distal end of the catheter. 14. The system of any of the preceding claims where the controller is configured to receive at least one signal from one or more sensors disposed near the distal end of the catheter. 15. The system of any of the preceding claims where the controller is configured to: perform one or more measurements of the blood pressure after infiisate is infused to a vessel via the catheter at an infiisate flow rate; and perform a calculation of the dynamic microvascular resistance at one of more calculations of the microvascular resistance at different times over a time period, the microvascular resistance being calculated by dividing the pressure measurement value by a value of infiisate volume flow rate and over multiple volume flow rates and measured pressure values. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the controller is further configured to determine change in a level of an MVO over the time period based on at least a dynamic microvascular resistance measurement performed at the different times. 17. The system of claim 15, wherein the dynamic microvascular resistance is adjusted for the coronary wedge pressure. 18. The system of claim 17, wherein any value of the coronary wedge pressure is used for the adjustment: mean, systolic, diastolic or any time dependent parameter from the coronary wedge pressure.
